title: Three-things manifest — skill, sex, people
html:
TL;DR

  • Late-October-2021 three line manifest from Yahir's freshman fall at OSU: learn a great skill by end of school, get laid by end of term, meet great people every day.
  • Closes with the nothing to lose line — "I have nothing to lose, thus be brave enough to try" — the core decision-rule of the Grants Pass to Corvallis transition.
  • Sits in the dense October-2021 cluster alongside Tesla aspiration, Tesla vs Edison, Buffett vs Bezos — Yahir is rapid-firing life-frame notes that month.
  • Combines the daily/term/year time-horizon stack — meeting people every day, sex by term, skill by year — a nested cadence framing of self-progress.
  • Maps to the skill as vocation thread (skill = career), the attraction thread (sex = vitality), and the network thread (people = leverage) — the three vectors that recur across the Austin thesis five years later.

Note

Three desires, one operating premise. Yahir at 18, freshman at Oregon State, stripping ambition down to its three primal channels — do something well, fuck someone, find your people — and ending with the nothing to lose permission slip. The brevity is the point; this is a letter to self in compressed form, the kind of thing he'll re-encounter in the junk drawer years later and recognize.

Connections

  • — form
  • no runway discipline — closing line
  • markers of development — nested time horizons
  • the austin thesis — skill/people/vitality re-articulated five years later

You're ready, but know you can make mistakes — title-as-permission-slip
